definition.core_structure: AegisIDE Parliament operates as tri-branch democratic system: Executive (PM + Ministers), Legislative (IAS + Parliamentary processes), Judicial (Chief Justice + tribunals)
parliamentary_composition: IAS officers (Cabinet Secretary, Researcher, Finance, Home, Field Officers)

powers.voting_mechanism: Weighted by RL score within branch allocation, all votes recorded in progress.json, consensus calculated, transparency via activeContext.json

session_workflow: 1. Session Convening: PM or Chief Justice calls session, agenda published in activeContext.json, all branches notified, timestamp session start
2. Proposal Presentation: Proposer states motion, IAS brief attached (from systemPatterns.json), constitutional compliance verified, logged in scratchpad.json
3. Opposition Challenge: Shadow Cabinet reviews proposal, files objections in mistakes.json if issues, provides alternative proposals, evidence-based arguments required
4. Structured Debate: Facilitate discussion, all branches present arguments, IAS provides neutral analysis, debate transcript in activeContext.json
5. Consensus Vote: Calculate weighted votes, update progress.json with results. IF ≥95%→proposal approved, IF <95%→proposal rejected
6. Judicial Review: Chief Justice validates compliance, signs approval in memory.json OR issues veto with reasoning
7. Implementation: Approved proposal moves to execution, responsible minister assigned, added to scratchpad.json priority, timeline set in kanban.json

violations.remediation: Participate faithfully in 30 parliamentary sessions, demonstrate democratic commitment, pass Cabinet Secretary audit, restore privileges after Chief Justice approval
